Person_Visionary: Scythian; Saka; and related steppe cultures
Location: Pontic Steppe / Central Asia (Ukraine through Kazakhstan to Altai Mountains)
Source_Text: Scythian metalwork (Pazyryk; Kul Oba; Tolstaya Mogila); Rolle 'The World of the Scythians' (1989); Anthony 'The Horse the Wheel and Language' (2007)
Description: Steppe world tree attested across Eurasia from Black Sea to Mongolia: tree with eagle at crown; serpent/dragon at root; antithetical flanking animals (stags; horses; griffins); tree as cosmic axis connecting shamanic upper/middle/lower worlds; shaman ascends the world tree in trance; eagle = upper-world divine messenger; serpent = chthonic power. In Saka/Scythian art the eagle-serpent battle is one of the defining iconographic themes — directly parallel to Garuda-Naga in Indian tradition. Structurally identical to Mesopotamian sacred tree and Zoroastrian Gaokerena.
Key_Motifs: World tree; eagle at crown; serpent/dragon at root; flanking animals; shamanic three-world ascent; axis mundi; eagle-serpent battle; pan-Eurasian PIE substrate
Parallel_Systems: Mesopotamian Tree (direct iconographic parallel; Scythians in contact with Assyria from 8th c. BCE – Tier B); Hekhalot (shamanic ascent through levels = Merkabah mysticism same structure); Kundalini (eagle-crown = sahasrara; serpent-root = muladhara kundalini – Tier C); Zoroastrian (Saena/Simurgh in Gaokerena = same eagle-in-tree); Norse Yggdrasil (identical eagle/serpent structure confirming PIE substrate)
Scholarly_Source: Rolle 'The World of the Scythians' (University of California Press 1989); Anthony 'The Horse the Wheel and Language' (2007); Eliade 'Shamanism: Archaic Techniques of Ecstasy' (1964) ch. 8
Notes: Steppe world tree is pan-Eurasian and predates most textual traditions; eagle-crown / serpent-root polarity is the defining pan-Eurasian world-tree structure; confirms Proto-Indo-European substrate for the motif

Title_Event: Yggdrasil – Norse Cosmic Tree; Odin's Nine-Night Self-Sacrifice; Nine Worlds
Person_Visionary: Norse tradition; codified in Prose Edda (Snorri Sturluson c. 1220 CE)
Source_Text: Prose Edda (Gylfaginning ch. 15–16); Poetic Edda (Völuspá); Davidson 'Gods and Myths of Northern Europe' (1964)
Description: Yggdrasil (Odin's gallows-tree / 'steed of the Terrible One') = cosmic ash connecting nine worlds; three roots reach to: Asgard (Urðarbrunnr well of fate); Jotunheim (Mimir's well of wisdom); Niflheim (Hvergelmir spring with dragon Niðhöggr gnawing the root). Eagle at crown; Niðhöggr at root; squirrel Ratatoskr carries messages up and down. Odin hangs for nine nights to gain the runes (esoteric knowledge through voluntary ordeal on the world-axis). Mimir's well of wisdom at the root = Ea's Apsu as wisdom-ocean (Mesopotamian structural parallel per Parpola).
Key_Motifs: Nine worlds; three roots; eagle at crown; Niðhöggr serpent at root; Odin nine-night ordeal; runes as gained esoteric knowledge; Mimir's well of wisdom
Parallel_Systems: Steppe World Tree (structurally identical eagle/serpent polarity – PIE shared substrate); Mesopotamian Tree (Mimir's well = Ea's Apsu wisdom-ocean; Odin hanging = king-as-Tree mystical ordeal); Kundalini (Odin's nine-night ordeal on tree = tapas/kundalini activation through sustained suffering – Tier C); Hekhalot (runes as passwords obtained through divine ordeal – Tier C)
Scholarly_Source: Davidson 'Gods and Myths of Northern Europe' (Penguin 1964); Simek 'Dictionary of Northern Mythology' (1993); Turville-Petre 'Myth and Religion of the North' (1964)
Notes: Yggdrasil is the most complete world-tree system in European tradition; eagle-serpent polarity confirms pan-Eurasian PIE pattern; Odin hanging = world-axis self-sacrifice shares theology with crucified Christ and Osiris-in-tree

Title_Event: Daoist Microcosmic Orbit (Xiao Zhoutian) – Chinese Kundalini Equivalent
Person_Visionary: Daoist inner alchemy (neidan) tradition; Yellow Emperor tradition
Source_Text: Huang Di Nei Jing (c. 300–100 BCE); 'The Secret of the Golden Flower' (Taiyi Jinhua Zongzhi); Ge Hong 'Baopuzi' (c. 320 CE); Cleary tr. (1991)
Description: Microcosmic orbit (small heavenly circulation = Xiao Zhoutian): qi circulates through two primary channels: (1) Du Mai (Governing Vessel) = ascending along spine from tailbone (Huiyin) to crown (Baihui) = structurally equivalent to sushumna/kundalini rising; (2) Ren Mai (Conception Vessel) = descending from crown down front to perineum = completing the circuit. Three dan tian (cinnabar fields) as energy centers: lower dan tian (belly/navel) = muladhara equivalent; middle dan tian (heart) = anahata equivalent; upper dan tian (crown/third eye) = sahasrara equivalent. 'Return to the source' = union with Dao = kundalini reaching the crown.
Key_Motifs: Du Mai (spine-ascending = sushumna); Ren Mai (front-descending = circuit completion); three dan tian (lower/middle/upper = three chakra zones); qi circulation; inner alchemy; return to source
Parallel_Systems: Kundalini (Du Mai = sushumna; three dan tian = lower-middle-upper chakra zones; microcosmic orbit = kundalini body-circuit – Tier A for structural parallel; independent development); Hekhalot (ascending channel to the divine = same vertical ascent motif); Stupa (three dan tian = three main stupa architectural levels)
Scholarly_Source: Cleary 'The Secret of the Golden Flower' (1991); Despeux 'Taoisme et corps humain' (1994); Needham 'Science and Civilisation in China' vol. 5 pt. 5 (1983)
Notes: Du Mai/Ren Mai circuit = most structurally direct non-Indian parallel to kundalini; three dan tian = three-zone chakra system; independent development is Tier A; causal connection to Indian system is Tier C

Title_Event: Asherah – Sacred Tree / Serpent Goddess as Living Energy Axis in Israelite Religion
Person_Visionary: Asherah cult; Canaanite tradition; Israelite religion (before Deuteronomistic reform)
Source_Text: Ugaritic texts KTU 1.1–6; 1 Kgs 14:23; 2 Kgs 17:10; Dt 16:21; Hadley 'The Cult of Asherah in Ancient Israel and Judah' (Cambridge UP 2000)
Description: Asherah = Canaanite mother goddess symbolized by a wooden pole or stylized tree (asherah); often depicted as a nude figure with snakes and flanking animals; the asherah pole stood beside Yahweh's altar in the Jerusalem Temple until the 7th c. BCE Deuteronomistic reform; Lady of the Sea; mother of 70 gods; her tree = axis connecting heaven and earth. Her serpent association links her to Egyptian Wadjet and Babylonian Ishtar/Tiamat = she IS the Sefirah Tiferet/Ishtar on Parpola's Mesopotamian Tree diagram. Her suppression by Deuteronomistic reformers = the erasure of the sacred tree / feminine from Israelite religion that Hekhalot mysticism arguably seeks to recover through the Shekhinah.
Key_Motifs: Asherah pole (sacred tree); nude goddess; flanking animals; serpent association; Lady of the Sea; mother of 70 gods; prohibited in Deuteronomistic reform; Shekhinah as continuation
Parallel_Systems: OT Visions (Asherah's suppression is the backdrop to Hekhalot's recovery of Shekhinah as feminine divine presence – Tier B); Mesopotamian Tree (Asherah = Canaanite form of Babylonian Ishtar = Tiferet on Parpola's diagram – Tier B); Kundalini (Asherah as Shakti goddess = divine feminine energy axis – Tier C)
Scholarly_Source: Hadley 'The Cult of Asherah in Ancient Israel and Judah' (Cambridge UP 2000); Smith 'The Early History of God' 2nd ed. (2002) ch. 4; Dever 'Did God Have a Wife?' (2005)
Notes: Asherah pole = one of the best-documented sacred objects in biblical archaeology (Tier A); identification with Babylonian Ishtar/Tiferet on Parpola's Tree is Tier B; the Asherah→Shekhinah continuity in Jewish mysticism is a major scholarly discussion (Tier B)

Title_Event: Mandaean Lightworld Ascent – Seven Sky-Gates / Password-Guarded Heavens / Living Water Column
Person_Visionary: Mandaean tradition (Nasoreai priests; John the Baptist community)
Location: Lower Mesopotamia / southern Iraq and Iran (Aramaic-speaking)
Source_Text: Ginza Rba (The Great Treasure; Lidzbarski ed. 1925); Mandaean Book of the Zodiac; Buckley 'The Mandaeans: Ancient Texts and Modern People' (AOS 2002)
Description: Mandaean system: soul ascends through seven planetary heavens (matharthas) back to the Lightworld (Alma d-Nhura); each heaven guarded by Uthra (angelic beings) requiring passwords; soul must demonstrate purity; the Lightworld = source of living water and divine light; John the Baptist revered as prophet; baptism (masbuta) in flowing water = repeated ritual of ascent/purification; the light-column (stauros = pillar of light) = axis of return. Structurally the closest geographic and theological parallel to Hekhalot outside Jewish tradition — both are Mesopotamian-area systems with seven-heaven/seven-hall angelic gatekeeping and password requirements.
Key_Motifs: Seven planetary heavens; password-guarded angelic gates; Uthra; Lightworld; living water; light-pillar (stauros); John the Baptist; baptism as repeated ascent
Parallel_Systems: Hekhalot (seven heavens with angelic gatekeepers requiring passwords = structural twin of seven Hekhalot halls – Tier A parallel); OT Visions (John the Baptist tradition; NT heavenly visions); Kundalini (light-pillar/stauros = subtle body central channel – Tier C)
Scholarly_Source: Buckley 'The Mandaeans: Ancient Texts and Modern People' (AOS 2002); Drower 'The Mandaeans of Iraq and Iran' (1937); Scholem 'Jewish Gnosticism, Merkabah Mysticism' (1960) pp. 44–56
Notes: Mandaean system is the closest geographic and structural parallel to Hekhalot outside Jewish tradition; direct Hekhalot influence OR common Babylonian source both plausible; the password-guarding structure is strikingly specific and unlikely to be coincidental

Title_Event: Caduceus – Hermetic Serpent-Staff as Western Kundalini Diagram (Ida / Pingala / Sushumna)
Source_Text: Corpus Hermeticum (Nock & Festugière ed. 1945); Homeric Hymn to Hermes; healing-serpent-staff-and-pillar.csv; Parpola JNES 52/3 (1993) n. 68
Description: The caduceus (kerykeion) = Hermes's staff with two intertwined serpents topped by wings; the two serpents = polar opposites (ida/pingala; active/passive; sun/moon; mercy/judgment); the central staff = sushumna = Pillar of Equilibrium on the Kabbalistic Tree; the wings at top = sahasrara / crown / Keter; the intertwining creates the exact ida-pingala pattern of kundalini physiology. The caduceus appears as a healing and psychopomp symbol — guide of souls = same function as the angelus interpres in Hekhalot (angelic guide at each hall). In Parpola's diagram Hermes = Nabu/Mercury = Netzah (Victory/Messenger) on the divine tree; apkallu figures holding pine cones = same position on the Assyrian Tree.
Key_Motifs: Two intertwined serpents (ida/pingala); central staff (sushumna); wings at top (sahasrara/Keter); psychopomp function; healing; polar opposites unified on central axis; Nabu-Hermes = Netzah
Parallel_Systems: Kundalini (caduceus = Western kundalini diagram: two serpents = ida/pingala; staff = sushumna; wings = sahasrara – Tier B; structural parallel widely accepted in comparative mysticism); Hekhalot (Metatron/Hermes as angelic guide-of-souls parallel); Mesopotamian Tree (apkallu with pine cone = Nabu-Hermes position on Parpola's Tree); OT Visions (Moses' brazen serpent Nehushtan = staff-with-serpent precursor)
Scholarly_Source: Kingsley 'Ancient Philosophy Mystery and Magic: Empedocles and Pythagorean Tradition' (OUP 1995); healing-serpent-staff-and-pillar.csv; Parpola JNES 52/3 (1993) n. 68
Notes: Caduceus-as-kundalini is the standard comparative mysticism argument (Tier B); structural parallel visually undeniable; functional psychopomp-angelus interpres parallel strengthens the case; causal connection between Greek and Indian systems unproven
